comment_text.sn20.11:1.2: Similar to SN 17.8, but somehow even more brutal in its application.

root_text.sn20.11:1.2: “Assuttha no tumhe, bhikkhave, rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ jarasiṅgālassa vassamānassā”ti?
root_text.sn20.11:1.4: “Eso kho, bhikkhave, jarasiṅgālo ukkaṇḍakena nāma rogajātena phuṭṭho.
root_text.sn20.11:1.5: So yena yena icchati tena tena gacchati;
root_text.sn20.11:1.6: yattha yattha icchati tattha tattha tiṭṭhati;
root_text.sn20.11:1.7: yattha yattha icchati tattha tattha nisīdati;
root_text.sn20.11:1.8: yattha yattha icchati tattha tattha nipajjati;
root_text.sn20.11:1.10: Sādhu khvassa, bhikkhave, yaṁ idhekacco sakyaputtiyapaṭiñño evarūpampi attabhāvapaṭilābhaṁ paṭisaṁvediyetha.

translation_text.sn20.11:1.2: “Mendicants, did you hear an old jackal howling at the crack of dawn?”
translation_text.sn20.11:1.4: “That old jackal has the disease called mange.
translation_text.sn20.11:1.5: Yet it still goes where it wants,
translation_text.sn20.11:1.8: and lies down where it wants.
translation_text.sn20.11:1.9: And the cool breeze still blows on it.
translation_text.sn20.11:1.10: A certain person here who claims to follow the Sakyan would be lucky to experience even such an incarnation.
translation_text.sn20.11:1.11: So you should train like this:
